critic_raw:
All normalization and scaling transformers are fit on the full dataset before splitting. For a longitudinal progression study, this includes normalizing HbA1c, blood pressure, and eGFR values against a global mean that incorporates test-period patient measurements. This is direct test-set contamination.

The split strategy is stratified random sampling on a longitudinal patient progression dataset where the outcome requires a baseline visit and a follow-up visit at 12+ months. Randomly splitting this dataset means that a patient's baseline record can appear in training while their follow-up (or vice versa) appears in test. More critically, since the label is defined by comparing baseline to follow-up, and the model predicts from baseline features, any information about follow-up status that is correlated with baseline clinical measurements (which is what the model is trying to learn) will be distributed across both sets. If the same patient is split, the model has partial information about that patient's progression trajectory.

The follow-up interval (12–36 months) is included as a meta-feature in both the model and the baseline. This feature encodes how much time the patient had to progress — patients with 36-month follow-up have 3x as long to progress as patients with 12-month follow-up. Including follow-up interval as a feature and using it for stratification ensures class balance, but stratification balances interval across sets without removing the model's ability to use this as a shortcut: a model could learn to predict 'longer follow-up → more likely to progress' rather than learning the clinical signal.

The baseline uses only tabular EHR features while the proposed model uses both the CNN on retinal images AND tabular features. The comparison tests whether retinal imaging adds incremental value over tabular features alone, not whether the CNN architecture is superior to logistic regression architecture. For a fair architectural test, logistic regression should also have access to image-derived features (e.g., hand-crafted retinal features or pretrained CNN embeddings), or the CNN should be restricted to images only.
all_issues_raised: Normalization transformers are fit on the full dataset before splitting, leaking test-period patient lab values and demographics into the scaling applied to training data.

Stratified random sampling on a longitudinal dataset where labels require matched baseline-follow-up pairs allows the same patient's baseline to appear in training and follow-up in test, potentially distributing partial trajectory information across splits.

Follow-up interval (12–36 months) is included as a model feature, providing a direct shortcut for progression prediction that does not reflect clinical signal — patients with longer follow-up have more time to progress regardless of clinical risk factors.
The CNN baseline comparison is confounded by data modality: CNN receives images plus tabular while logistic regression receives tabular only — the comparison tests imaging value, not architecture.

Stratified random sampling on a longitudinal dataset where labels require matched baseline-follow-up pairs allows the same patient's baseline to appear in training and follow-up in test, potentially distributing partial trajectory information across splits.

Follow-up interval (12–36 months) is included as a model feature, providing a direct shortcut for progression prediction that does not reflect clinical signal — patients with longer follow-up have more time to progress regardless of clinical risk factors.
The CNN baseline comparison is confounded by data modality: CNN receives images plus tabular while logistic regression receives tabular only — the comparison tests imaging value, not architecture.
